nodes.name: Claim Next Batch (API)
nodes.notes: Replaces the old Reap Stale Processing Items / Count In-Progress Items / Check Capacity / Claim Next Batch Postgres+Code+If nodes -- ingest-worker's /ai-queue/claim now does all four steps in one call (reap stale, count in-progress, compute capacity, atomically claim up to parallel_limit crop_status='done' rows FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED). One shared queue across all object_types (not claimed separately per type), newest event first -- older backlog only gets swept up once capacity isn't all consumed by newer rows. Returns {events: [...]} -- empty if no capacity or no work. max_age_hours (set to 6 below) means rows older than 6h are never claimed at all -- a safety valve so a stale backlog can't eat capacity that could go to fresher events. source=visits claims one representative raw_event per distinct object type a Frigate review grouped into a visit (so a car and a person in the same visit both get analyzed, but re-tracked duplicates of the same object -- tracker re-ID, label flicker -- still collapse to just one). require_thumb_crop=true (set below) makes this GUARANTEE crop_image_base64 is always the visit's own composite preview grid (crop.build_visit_preview -- 4 frames sampled across the visit's clip, combined into one image) -- IMPORTANT TRADE-OFF: combined with source=visits, require_thumb_crop=true also forces `AND visit_id IS NOT NULL`, so a raw_event that was never grouped into a visit at all is NEVER claimed by this workflow anymore (previously this workflow was a strict superset of the old events-only mode -- that's no longer true with require_thumb_crop on; add visits_only=true explicitly if you want that scoping to be unambiguous in this config too, though it's already implied). The other real cost is latency: claims now wait for the review to close AND the preview-grid worker to finish (VISIT_THUMB_CROP_ENABLED must be on), not just crop_status='done' -- a visit whose grid isn't ready yet simply keeps waiting rather than falling back to the representative event's own single-frame crop.

nodes.name: Split Claimed Events
nodes.notes: Splits the {events: [...]} array from Claim Next Batch (API) into one n8n item per claimed event -- an HTTP node's raw JSON response doesn't reliably auto-split into items across n8n versions, so this is explicit rather than relied-upon.

nodes.name: Is Person?
nodes.notes: Routes each claimed row by its object type -- person goes to the person-description prompt/table, everything else (car/truck, guaranteed by Claim Next Batch (API)'s object_types param) goes to the vehicle-attributes+plate prompt/table.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { "messages": [ { "role": "user", "content": [ { "type": "text", "text": "This image is a 2x2 grid of 4 frames of the SAME person captured moments apart during one sighting (not 4 different people) -- use all 4 panels together, since a detail unclear in one may be visible in another. Describe this person's clothing colors and what they appear to be doing across the sequence, in one short sentence. No speculation about identity. Do not mention that the image is a grid." }, { "type": "image_url", "image_url": { "url": "data:image/jpeg;base64," + $json.crop_image_base64 } } ] } ], "temperature": 0 } }}

nodes.name: Build Embedding Text (Person)
nodes.notes: One line of text to embed, from the same description field the person gets analyzed into -- nothing new to compute, just the input the embedding model needs.

parameters.jsCode:
const p = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: { ...p, embed_text: p.description || '' } }];

nodes.name: Call Embedding Model (Person)
nodes.notes: nomic-embed-text-v1.5 (768 dims) via its own llama_slot_proxy slot -- same unauthenticated-on-the-LAN convention as the VLM calls above, just a different slot/path. continueErrorOutput on purpose: an embedding failure shouldn't lose an already-computed sighting -- both outputs feed Insert Person Sighting (API), which falls back to embedding=null via optional chaining if this errored.

nodes.name: Insert Person Sighting (API)
nodes.notes: Replaces the old Insert Person Sighting + Mark Done Postgres node pair -- ingest-worker's POST /sightings/persons inserts the sighting and marks ai_status='done' in one DB transaction. embedding pulls from Call Embedding Model (Person) explicitly (not bare $json) since that's the immediately preceding node now -- ?? null covers both a clean 'no embedding' case and that call having errored.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { "raw_event_id": $('Parse VLM Response (Person)').item.json.raw_event_id, "description": $('Parse VLM Response (Person)').item.json.description, "notes": $('Parse VLM Response (Person)').item.json.notes, "embedding": $('Call Embedding Model (Person)').item.json.data?.[0]?.embedding ?? null } }}

nodes.name: Call Qwen (Attributes + Plate)
nodes.notes: Merged attributes + plate OCR into one call for speed (one ~45-90s LLM round trip per vehicle event instead of two). Trade-off: Qwen isn't a dedicated OCR specialist, so plate_text_llm may be less accurate than a separate-OCR-model approach -- plate_text_frigate (Frigate's own LPR read) is kept alongside it as a cross-check either way.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { "messages": [ { "role": "user", "content": [ { "type": "text", "text": "This image is a 2x2 grid of 4 frames of the SAME vehicle captured moments apart during one sighting (not 4 different vehicles) -- use all 4 panels together to give the single most confident answer, since a detail unclear in one panel (angle, glare, motion blur) may be legible in another, e.g. the plate. Respond with ONLY a JSON object, no other text before or after it, no markdown, no explanation, and do not mention that the image is a grid: {\"color\": string, \"body_type\": string (e.g. sedan/suv/truck/van/pickup/minivan), \"make\": string or null if unsure, \"make_confidence\": \"low\"|\"medium\"|\"high\", \"model\": string or null if unsure (e.g. \"F-150\", \"Camry\", \"Model 3\" -- the specific model, not just the make), \"model_confidence\": \"low\"|\"medium\"|\"high\", \"notable_features\": string or null, a short phrase (under 10 words) for anything visually distinctive -- roof rack, ladder rack, trailer hitch, bike rack, tinted windows, decals or commercial branding/text, visible damage or rust, unusual color/wrap, aftermarket wheels/lift kit -- use null if the vehicle looks unremarkable, \"plate_text\": string or null}. plate_text must be ONLY the plate's characters (e.g. \"10MG407\") with no spaces, punctuation, labels, or reasoning -- if no plate is visible or legible in any panel, use null. Do not describe your process." }, { "type": "image_url", "image_url": { "url": "data:image/jpeg;base64," + $json.crop_image_base64 } } ] } ], "temperature": 0 } }}

nodes.name: Build Embedding Text (Vehicle)
nodes.notes: Same color/body/make/model + notable_features + plate combination report.py's _vehicle_summary already uses for the alerts report's one-line description -- embedding the same text a human would read about this sighting, not a separately-invented format.

nodes.name: Call Embedding Model (Vehicle)
nodes.notes: Same embedding slot as Call Embedding Model (Person). continueErrorOutput on purpose -- an embedding failure shouldn't lose an already-computed vehicle sighting; both outputs feed Insert Vehicle Sighting (API), which falls back to embedding=null via optional chaining if this errored.

nodes.name: Insert Vehicle Sighting (API)
nodes.notes: Replaces the old Insert Vehicle Sighting + Mark Done Postgres node pair -- ingest-worker's POST /sightings/vehicles inserts the sighting and marks ai_status='done' in one DB transaction. embedding pulls from Call Embedding Model (Vehicle) explicitly (not bare $json) since that's the immediately preceding node now -- ?? null covers both a clean 'no embedding' case and that call having errored.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { "raw_event_id": $('Parse VLM Responses (Vehicle)').item.json.raw_event_id, "color": $('Parse VLM Responses (Vehicle)').item.json.color, "body_type": $('Parse VLM Responses (Vehicle)').item.json.body_type, "make_guess": $('Parse VLM Responses (Vehicle)').item.json.make_guess, "make_confidence": $('Parse VLM Responses (Vehicle)').item.json.make_confidence, "model_guess": $('Parse VLM Responses (Vehicle)').item.json.model_guess, "model_confidence": $('Parse VLM Responses (Vehicle)').item.json.model_confidence, "notable_features": $('Parse VLM Responses (Vehicle)').item.json.notable_features, "plate_text_llm": $('Parse VLM Responses (Vehicle)').item.json.plate_text_llm, "plate_text_frigate": $('Parse VLM Responses (Vehicle)').item.json.plate_text_frigate, "plate_confidence": $('Parse VLM Responses (Vehicle)').item.json.plate_confidence, "notes": $('Parse VLM Responses (Vehicle)').item.json.notes, "embedding": $('Call Embedding Model (Vehicle)').item.json.data?.[0]?.embedding ?? null } }}

nodes.name: Handle Failure (API)
nodes.notes: Any VLM call's error branch (person or vehicle path) lands here. ingest-worker's POST /ai-queue/{id}/fail runs the same retry-or-fail-with-cap logic the old Handle Failure (Retry or Fail) Postgres node did: below max_attempts goes back to ai_status='retry' (picked up again on a future run, not looped in this execution), at/above it becomes 'failed' (terminal).
